Toss Update: PBKS bowl first in New Chandigarh! 




 

Pitch Report by Pommie Mbangwa and Michael Clarke:

"The conditions feel outstanding and this is a fantastic stadium with a smooth outfield right up to the boundary, and while we will get to the pitch shortly it already looks a good one. In terms of dimensions, one side is sixty four metres, the other around seventy, straight is seventy four with a seventy five metre pocket, which means you have to hit cleanly or run hard in those areas. Michael Clarke feels this is a beautiful surface with a rock hard strip underneath, slightly dry grass and expects minimal spin with no major change whether teams bat or bowl first. This looks like a genuine high-scoring venue with Clarke eyeing totals above two hundred and the average batting first here sitting around two one seven. With little dew expected, teams can choose either option since some prefer chasing while others like to set a total."



Players to Watch
For Punjab Kings, Prabhsimran Singh remains a vital spark at the top. His explosive starts have often tilted games early, and the numbers underline his impact. Across 51 IPL innings, he has piled up 1305 runs at a striking rate of 152, registering eight scores over fifty, including seven half-centuries and a memorable century. When he fires, PBKS tend to follow.

Gujarat Titans, meanwhile, draw assurance from the consistency of Sai Sudharsan. Reliability has been his trademark, with 1793 runs amassed from just 40 matches. Twelve fifties and two hundreds speak of his appetite for big scores, all delivered at a healthy strike rate of 146. For GT, his presence at the crease often forms the backbone of their batting effort.



Venue at a Glance
Since 2024, the New Chandigarh venue has hosted 11 IPL matches and the numbers suggest a fine balance with a slight tilt towards teams batting first. Sides setting a target have won six games, while chasing teams have prevailed five times. Pace bowlers have enjoyed greater influence here, claiming 67 percent of the wickets, with spinners accounting for the remaining 33 percent. The powerplay has offered steady scoring opportunities, averaging around 53 runs, while the death overs have been tough on bowlers, who have gone at 10.6 an over. The average first innings score at this venue stands at 169, keeping contests competitive and tightly poised.



Head-to-Head in IPL
Punjab Kings and Gujarat Titans are locked in a perfectly balanced rivalry so far. In the six IPL encounters between the two sides, both teams have claimed three victories each, making it an even-steven contest heading into their next clash.




Punjab Kings and Gujarat Titans are set for an intriguing clash as two contrasting styles go head-to-head in the TATA IPL 2026. While PBKS thrive on explosive starts, GT prefer building a solid base before accelerating — a classic fire vs ice contest.

Punjab come into this game with continuity and confidence after narrowly missing the title last season. Their approach remains aggressive at the top, with Priyansh Arya and Prabhsimran Singh expected to take the attack to the opposition early. With a strong middle order featuring Shreyas Iyer, Marcus Stoinis and Nehal Wadhera, PBKS also have the depth to sustain momentum through the innings. Their bowling unit, led by Arshdeep Singh, Marco Jansen and Yuzvendra Chahal, offers both pace and spin variety.

Gujarat Titans, on the other hand, rely heavily on their consistent opening pair of Shubman Gill and Sai Sudharsan, who were among the most productive duos last season. Their ability to anchor the innings allows the middle order to play defined roles, though that remains an area GT will want more output from. With experienced names like Jos Buttler, Rahul Tewatia and Washington Sundar, they have the tools but need better consistency. Their bowling attack looks formidable, with Mohammed Siraj, Rashid Khan and Kagiso Rabada forming a strong core.

The pitch at Mullanpur is expected to offer a balanced contest. While big scores are possible, totals around 180-190 have often been competitive, bringing both batters and bowlers into play.

Ultimately, the match could be decided in the PowerPlay. If PBKS get their explosive start, they could dictate terms early. But if GT’s openers settle in, they have the structure to control the game. A tight contest awaits.
MatchSummary.PostMatchCommentary:
Cooper Connolly (Player of the Match):
"I wanted to focus on gaining experience by playing good cricket alongside world-class players, and it was nice to put on a show and start the season with a win. Before this game, I had a small reset with a couple of practice days at home and time with family and friends, which helped me come in feeling fresh. I see myself as more of a tempo player rather than someone who goes for constant big hits, and working with Ricky has been excellent in helping me find that rhythm early. When things got tight in the chase, I stayed relaxed and felt we were always in a good position with Marco and Xavier calm as well, which helped the dressing room. Facing Rashid for the first time was a great contest and contributing for the team makes me excited for the rest of the tournament."


 

THE N?E?X?T? BIG THING ??????#CooperConnolly pic.twitter.com/lOWAlCLYF9

— Punjab Kings (@PunjabKingsIPL) March 31, 2026

Shreyas Iyer (Punjab Kings Captain):
"My hand feels fine and pretty much the same as before, so I hope it stays that way and everything settles well. Cooper Connolly was outstanding and some of the shots he played were unbelievable, especially the clean backfoot six off Rashid which showed his confidence and ability. In the dugout I was icing my hand when we suddenly lost two wickets, but moments like that are part of the IPL and staying calm is important, especially when someone as composed as Cooper is set at the crease. Once we realised the pitch was slowing after the third over, the plan was to take the pace off early and use slower balls into the surface which worked for us. Vyshak has carried forward his form from last season, he thrives in big moments, and seeing captains trying aggressive fields like forward short leg is refreshing and good for the game."


 

Shubman Gill (Gujarat Titans Captain):
"I felt we bowled quite well overall but the last five or six overs did not go our way, and while batting we knew this was not a two hundred wicket with the talk in the middle being around one seventy or one eighty. The chase felt gettable at one stage but we kept losing wickets and did not bat through the final overs which made the difference. As the ball aged it became harder to hit straight and there was not much pace from the surface, while in the second innings the wet ball from rain meant it did not grip as much and I thought we still did a decent job with the runs available. Prasidh came in at the right time because we felt a skiddy quick could make an impact on a slow pitch and he did his role well, while Ashok keeps working hard and hopefully shows his skills consistently with the new ball. The message to the boys is simple, it was tough luck today but keep the intensity high and continue giving your best."


 

Punjab Kings emerged victorious after a tense and fluctuating contest, edging past Gujarat Titans by three wickets in a match where momentum refused to settle for long and scoring rates stayed relentlessly high.

Gujarat Titans put up 162 for 6, a total built steadily rather than spectacularly. Shubman Gill’s 39 off 27 set the platform, Jos Buttler added 38 from 33, and Glenn Phillips kept the innings moving with 25 from 17. Punjab’s bowlers prevented any late surge, led by Vijaykumar Vyshak’s 3 for 34 and Yuzvendra Chahal’s economical 2 for 28, restricting Gujarat to just 43 runs in the final five overs.

Punjab’s chase began with clear intent and control. By the 14th over, they were firmly ahead, riding on Cooper Connolly’s debut knock and Prabhsimran Singh’s 37, with the pair adding 76 and keeping the asking rate manageable. At that stage, Punjab looked in command.

The match turned sharply in the 15th over when Prasidh Krishna struck twice, dragging Gujarat back into the contest and swinging the win probability from around 30 percent to nearly 52 percent. Just as pressure peaked, Marco Jansen and Connolly responded with authority, reclaiming momentum and calming the chase.

From there on, Punjab did not look back. Connolly remained unbeaten on 72 off 44, guiding the side home in 19.1 overs. With both teams scoring at over nine a piece for much of the game, it was a thriller shaped by sharp momentum shifts and decisive individual moments.
